title: centaurea × lieblingii
intro: Centaurea × lieblingii, also known as mountain cornflower, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the aster family and is native to the mountains of the western United States and Canada. It has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and protect against cell damage. The plant's health benefits include its ability to support digestive health, reduce anxiety and stress, and act as an antiparasitic agent. Centaurea × lieblingii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a versatile and accessible herbal remedy.
